element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary mechanic; required to kill enemies instead of jumping on them (distinguishing feature from typical platformers)
Used to purchase items from shops throughout islands
Purchase spells and ability upgrades with collected bones
Launched from club; up to 3 can shoot simultaneously; available via shop purchase
Instant death; Big Nose cannot swim; introduces themed island danger
Escalated difficulty; enemy density and trap complexity increase

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform sections between hazard zones
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sustained high difficulty with shop breaks; difficulty curve aggressive and unforgiving (no mid-run checkpoints, single-hit deaths without stone upgrade)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Paradise Island introduces core mechanics (club combat, bone collection, shopping) in controlled environment; foundational challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster Island escalates enemy frequency and trap complexity; introduces themed hazards (lava/water)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Terror Island and Chaos Island represent maximum difficulty concentration with combined hazards and likely enemy density increases
notable_spikes: Transition from Paradise Island to Monster Island (hazard type introduction)
Terror Island introduction (thematic and mechanical complexity spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized into 4 named island worlds (Paradise, Monster, Terror, Chaos) with progressive difficulty; each island contains multiple levels. Structure mirrors traditional platformer world organization with thematic escalation

Bones collected during platforming sections enable purchase of ability upgrades and spells that may gate access to later content or difficulty mitigation (stone projectile upgrade critical for survival without single-hit deaths)

Core difficulty mechanic: Big Nose dies in single hit unless protected by stone upgrade; no checkpoint restarts upon game over (must restart from level 1); creates high-stakes resource management around stone purchases

sources_used: tcrf - Big Nose the Caveman (The Cutting Room Floor)
